NKJV.1: But know this, that in the last days perilous times will come:
NKJV.2: For men will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
NKJV.3: unloving, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, brutal, despisers of good,
NKJV.4: traitors, headstrong, haughty, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
NKJV.5: having a form of godliness but denying its power. And from such people turn away!
NKJV.6: For of this sort are those who creep into households and make captives of gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,
NKJV.7: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
NKJV.8: Now as Jannes and Jambres resisted Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, disapproved concerning the faith;
NKJV.9: but they will progress no further, for their folly will be manifest to all, as theirs also was.
NKJV.10: But you have carefully followed my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, love, perseverance,
NKJV.11: persecutions, afflictions, which happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra—what persecutions I endured. And out of them all the Lord delivered me.
NKJV.12: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
NKJV.13: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.
NKJV.14: But you must continue in the things which you have learned and been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them,
NKJV.15: and that from childhood you have known the Holy Scriptures, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.16: All Scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness,
NKJV.17: that the man of God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.

ESV.1: But understand this, that in the last days there will come times of difficulty.
ESV.2: For people will be lovers of self, lovers of money, proud, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
ESV.3: heartless, unappeasable, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not loving good,
ESV.4: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
ESV.5: having the appearance of godliness, but denying its power. Avoid such people.
ESV.6: For among them are those who creep into households and capture weak women, burdened with sins and led astray by various passions,
ESV.7: always learning and never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
ESV.8: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men corrupted in mind and disqualified regarding the faith.
ESV.9: But they will not get very far, for their folly will be plain to all, as was that of those two men.
ESV.10: You, however, have followed my teaching, my conduct, my aim in life, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadfastness,
ESV.11: my persecutions and sufferings that happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, and at Lystra — which persecutions I endured; yet from them all the Lord rescued me.
ESV.12: Indeed, all who desire to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted,
ESV.13: while evil people and impostors will go on from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
ESV.14: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and have firmly believed, knowing from whom  you learned it
ESV.15: and how from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
ESV.16: All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
ESV.17: that the man of God  may be complete, equipped for every good work.

NASB.1: But realize this, that in the last days difficult times will come.
NASB.2: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, revilers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
NASB.3: unloving, irreconcilable, malicious gossips, without self-control, brutal, haters of good,
NASB.4: treacherous, reckless, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
NASB.5: holding to a form of godliness, although they have denied its power; Avoid such men as these.
NASB.6: For among them are those who enter into households and captivate weak women weighed down with sins, led on by various impulses,
NASB.7: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
NASB.8: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these [men] also oppose the truth, men of depraved mind, rejected in regard to the faith.
NASB.9: But they will not make further progress; for their folly will be obvious to all, just as Jannes's and Jambres's folly was also.
NASB.10: Now you followed my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, perseverance,
NASB.11: persecutions, [and] sufferings, such as happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium [and] at Lystra; what persecutions I endured, and out of them all the Lord rescued me!
NASB.12: Indeed, all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
NASB.13: But evil men and impostors will proceed [from bad] to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
NASB.14: You, however, continue in the things you have learned and become convinced of, knowing from whom you have learned [them],
NASB.15: and that from childhood you have known the sacred writings which are able to give you the wisdom that leads to salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
NASB.16: All Scripture is inspired by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, for training in righteousness;
NASB.17: so that the man of God may be adequate, equipped for every good work.

NLT.1: You should know this, Timothy, that in the last days there will be very difficult times.
NLT.2: For people will love only themselves and their money. They will be boastful and proud, scoffing at God, disobedient to their parents, and ungrateful. They will consider nothing sacred.
NLT.3: They will be unloving and unforgiving; they will slander others and have no self-control. They will be cruel and hate what is good.
NLT.4: They will betray their friends, be reckless, be puffed up with pride, and love pleasure rather than God.
NLT.5: They will act religious, but they will reject the power that could make them godly. Stay away from people like that!
NLT.6: They are the kind who work their way into people’s homes and win the confidence of vulnerable women who are burdened with the guilt of sin and controlled by various desires.
NLT.7: (Such women are forever following new teachings, but they are never able to understand the truth.)
NLT.8: These teachers oppose the truth just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. They have depraved minds and a counterfeit faith.
NLT.9: But they won’t get away with this for long. Someday everyone will recognize what fools they are, just as with Jannes and Jambres.
NLT.10: But you, Timothy, certainly know what I teach, and how I live, and what my purpose in life is. You know my faith, my patience, my love, and my endurance.
NLT.11: You know how much persecution and suffering I have endured. You know all about how I was persecuted in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra — but the Lord rescued me from all of it.
NLT.12: Yes, and everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
NLT.13: But evil people and impostors will flourish. They will deceive others and will themselves be deceived.
NLT.14: But you must remain faithful to the things you have been taught. You know they are true, for you know you can trust those who taught you.
NLT.15: You have been taught the holy Scriptures from childhood, and they have given you the wisdom to receive the salvation that comes by trusting in Christ Jesus.
NLT.16: All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we are wrong and teaches us to do what is right.
NLT.17: God uses it to prepare and equip his people to do every good work.

WEB.1: But know this, that in the last days, grievous times will come.
WEB.2: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
WEB.3: without natural affection, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, fierce, not lovers of good,
WEB.4: traitors, headstrong, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God;
WEB.5: holding a form of godliness, but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also.
WEB.6: For some of these are people who creep into houses, and take captive gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,
WEB.7: always learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
WEB.8: Even as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so do these also oppose the truth; men corrupted in mind, who concerning the faith, are rejected.
WEB.9: But they will proceed no further. For their folly will be evident to all men, as theirs also came to be.
WEB.10: But you did follow my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness,
WEB.11: persecutions, and sufferings: those things that happened to me at Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I endured those persecutions. The Lord delivered me out of them all.
WEB.12: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
WEB.13: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.
WEB.14: But you remain in the things which you have learned and have been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them.
WEB.15: From infancy, you have known the holy Scriptures which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith, which is in Christ Jesus.
WEB.16: Every Scripture is God-breathed and  profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness,
WEB.17: that the man of God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.

CSB17.1: But know this: Hard times will come in the last days. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.2: For people will be lovers of self, <sup>ⓐ</sup> lovers of money, <sup>ⓑ</sup> boastful, proud, <sup>ⓒ</sup> demeaning, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.3: unloving, irreconcilable, slanderers, <sup>ⓔ</sup> without self-control, brutal, without love for what is good, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.4: traitors, <sup>ⓖ</sup> reckless, <sup>ⓗ</sup> conceited, <sup>ⓘ</sup> lovers of pleasure <sup>ⓙ</sup> rather than lovers of God,
CSB17.5: holding to the form of godliness but denying its power. <sup>ⓚ</sup> Avoid these people. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.6: For among them are those who worm their way into households and deceive gullible women overwhelmed by sins and led astray by a variety of passions, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.7: always learning and never able to come to a knowledge of the truth. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.8: Just as Jannes and Jambres <sup>ⓐ</sup> resisted Moses, <sup>ⓑ</sup> so these also resist the truth. They are men who are corrupt in mind <sup>ⓒ</sup> and worthless <sup>ⓓ</sup> in regard to the faith.
CSB17.9: But they will not make further progress, for their foolishness will be clear to all, as was the foolishness of Jannes and Jambres.
CSB17.10: But you have followed my teaching, conduct, purpose, <sup>ⓔ</sup> faith, patience, love, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and endurance,
CSB17.11: along with the persecutions and sufferings <sup>ⓖ</sup> that came to me in Antioch, <sup>ⓗ</sup> Iconium, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and Lystra. <sup>ⓙ</sup> What persecutions I endured — and yet the Lord rescued me from them all.
CSB17.12: In fact, all who want to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.13: Evil people and impostors will become worse, deceiving <sup>ⓛ</sup> and being deceived.
CSB17.14: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and firmly believed. You know those who taught you, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.15: and you know that from infancy you have known the sacred Scriptures, <sup>ⓝ</sup> which are able to give you wisdom for salvation <sup>ⓐ</sup> through faith in Christ Jesus.
CSB17.16: All Scripture is inspired by God <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> and is profitable for teaching, for rebuking, for correcting, for training in righteousness,
CSB17.17: so that the man of God may be complete, equipped for every good work. <sup>ⓒ</sup>

AMP.1: But understand this, that in the last days dangerous times [of great stress and trouble] will come [difficult days that will be hard to bear].
AMP.2: For people will be lovers of self [narcissistic, self-focused], lovers of money [impelled by greed], boastful, arrogant, revilers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy and profane,
AMP.3: [and they will be] unloving [devoid of natural human affection, calloused and inhumane], irreconcilable, malicious gossips, devoid of self-control [intemperate, immoral], brutal, haters of good,
AMP.4: traitors, reckless, conceited, lovers of [sensual] pleasure rather than lovers of God,
AMP.5: holding to a form of [outward] godliness (religion), although they have denied its power [for their conduct nullifies their claim of faith]. Avoid such people and keep far away from them.
AMP.6: For among them are those who worm their way into homes and captivate morally weak and spiritually-dwarfed women weighed down by [the burden of their] sins, easily swayed by various impulses,
AMP.7: always learning and listening to anybody who will teach them, but never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
AMP.8: Just as Jannes and Jambres [the court magicians of Egypt] opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men of depraved mind, unqualified and worthless [as teachers] in regard to the faith.
AMP.9: But they will not get very far, for their meaningless nonsense and ignorance will become obvious to everyone, as was that of Jannes and Jambres.
AMP.10: Now you have diligently followed [my example, that is] my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness,
AMP.11: persecutions, and sufferings—such as happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, and at Lystra; what persecutions I endured, but the Lord rescued me from them all!
AMP.12: Indeed, all who delight in pursuing righteousness and are determined to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will be hunted and persecuted [because of their faith].
AMP.13: But evil men and impostors will go on from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
AMP.14: But as for you, continue in the things that you have learned and of which you are convinced [holding tightly to the truths], knowing from whom you learned them,
AMP.15: and how from childhood you have known the sacred writings (Hebrew Scriptures) which are able to give you the wisdom that leads to salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus [surrendering your entire self to Him and having absolute confidence in His wisdom, power and goodness].
AMP.16: All Scripture is God-breathed [given by divine inspiration] and is profitable for instruction, for conviction [of sin], for correction [of error and restoration to obedience], for training in righteousness [learning to live in conformity to God's will, both publicly and privately—behaving honorably with personal integrity and moral courage];
AMP.17: so that the man of God may be complete and proficient, outfitted and thoroughly equipped for every good work.

MSG.1: Don't be naive. There are difficult times ahead.
MSG.2: As the end approaches, people are going to be self-absorbed, money-hungry, self-promoting, stuck-up, profane, contemptuous of parents, crude, coarse,
MSG.3: dog-eat-dog, unbending, slanderers, impulsively wild, savage, cynical,
MSG.4: treacherous, ruthless, bloated windbags, addicted to lust, and allergic to God.
MSG.5: They'll make a show of religion, but behind the scenes they're animals. Stay clear of these people.
MSG.6: These are the kind of people who smooth-talk themselves into the homes of unstable and needy women and take advantage of them; women who, depressed by their sinfulness, take up with every new religious fad that calls itself "truth."
MSG.7: They get exploited every time and never really learn.
MSG.8: These men are like those old Egyptian frauds Jannes and Jambres, who challenged Moses. They were rejects from the faith, twisted in their thinking, defying truth itself.
MSG.9: But nothing will come of these latest impostors. Everyone will see through them, just as people saw through that Egyptian hoax.
MSG.10: You've been a good apprentice to me, a part of my teaching, my manner of life, direction, faith, steadiness, love, patience,
MSG.11: troubles, sufferings—suffering along with me in all the grief I had to put up with in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. And you also well know that God rescued me!
MSG.12: Anyone who wants to live all out for Christ is in for a lot of trouble; there's no getting around it.
MSG.13: Unscrupulous con men will continue to exploit the faith. They're as deceived as the people they lead astray. As long as they are out there, things can only get worse.
MSG.14: But don't let it faze you. Stick with what you learned and believed, sure of the integrity of your teachers—
MSG.15: why, you took in the sacred Scriptures with your mother's milk! There's nothing like the written Word of God for showing you the way to salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
MSG.16: Every part of Scripture is God-breathed and useful one way or another—showing us truth, exposing our rebellion, correcting our mistakes, training us to live God's way.
MSG.17: Through the Word we are put together and shaped up for the tasks God has for us.

NIV.1:
Godlessness in the Last Days
But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days.
NIV.2: People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
NIV.3: without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good,
NIV.4: treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God—
NIV.5: having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with them.
NIV.6: They are the kind who worm their way into homes and gain control over weak-willed women, who are loaded down with sins and are swayed by all kinds of evil desires,
NIV.7: always learning but never able to acknowledge the truth.
NIV.8: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so also these men oppose the truth—men of depraved minds, who, as far as the faith is concerned, are rejected.
NIV.9: But they will not get very far because, as in the case of those men, their folly will be clear to everyone.
NIV.10:
Paul’s Charge to Timothy
You, however, know all about my teaching, my way of life, my purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance,
NIV.11: persecutions, sufferings—what kinds of things happened to me in Antioch, Iconium and Lystra, the persecutions I endured. Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them.
NIV.12: In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted,
NIV.13: while evil men and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
NIV.14: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and have become convinced of, because you know those from whom you learned it,
NIV.15: and how from infancy you have known the holy Scriptures, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
NIV.16: All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness,
NIV.17: so that the man of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work.

NRSVCE.1: You must understand this, that in the last days distressing times will come.
NRSVCE.2: For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
NRSVCE.3: inhuman, implacable, slanderers, profligates, brutes, haters of good,
NRSVCE.4: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
NRSVCE.5: holding to the outward form of godliness but denying its power. Avoid them!
NRSVCE.6: For among them are those who make their way into households and captivate silly women, overwhelmed by their sins and swayed by all kinds of desires,
NRSVCE.7: who are always being instructed and can never arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
NRSVCE.8: As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people, of corrupt mind and counterfeit faith, also oppose the truth.
NRSVCE.9: But they will not make much progress, because, as in the case of those two men, their folly will become plain to everyone.
NRSVCE.10: Now you have observed my teaching, my conduct, my aim in life, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadfastness,
NRSVCE.11: my persecutions, and my suffering the things that happened to me in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. What persecutions I endured! Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them.
NRSVCE.12: Indeed, all who want to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
NRSVCE.13: But wicked people and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived.
NRSVCE.14: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and firmly believed, knowing from whom you learned it,
NRSVCE.15: and how from childhood you have known the sacred writings that are able to instruct you for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.16: All scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
NRSVCE.17: so that everyone who belongs to God may be proficient, equipped for every good work.

NET.1: But understand this, that in the last days difficult times will come.
NET.2: For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
NET.3: unloving, irreconcilable, slanderers, without self-control, savage, opposed to what is good,
NET.4: treacherous, reckless, conceited, loving pleasure rather than loving God.
NET.5: They will maintain the outward appearance of religion but will have repudiated its power. So avoid people like these.
NET.6: For some of these insinuate themselves into households and captivate weak women who are overwhelmed with sins and led along by various passions.
NET.7: Such women are always seeking instruction, yet never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
NET.8: And just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people — who have warped minds and are disqualified in the faith — also oppose the truth.
NET.9: But they will not go much further, for their foolishness will be obvious to everyone, just like it was with Jannes and Jambres.
NET.10: You, however, have followed my teaching, my way of life, my purpose, my faith, my patience, my love, my endurance,
NET.11: as well as the persecutions and sufferings that happened to me in Antioch, in Iconium, and in Lystra. I endured these persecutions and the Lord delivered me from them all.
NET.12: Now in fact all who want to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
NET.13: But evil people and charlatans will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived themselves.
NET.14: You, however, must continue in the things you have learned and are confident about. You know who taught you
NET.15: and how from infancy you have known the holy writings, which are able to give you wisdom for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
NET.16: Every scripture is inspired by God and useful for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
NET.17: that the person dedicated to God may be capable and equipped for every good work.
